metadata.purpose: Track which project rules are implemented, which SRD chunks support them, where they are implemented, and which tests verify them.

status_legend.implemented: Behavior is implemented in code and covered by at least one relevant test.
status_legend.partial: Behavior is implemented only in part, has known gaps, or mixes rule-backed and assumed logic.
status_legend.provisional: Behavior is implemented as an app policy or simplifying assumption and is not yet fully grounded in chunked rules.
status_legend.not_started: Relevant rule exists in the corpus, but deterministic backend support is not implemented yet.

rules.title: Attack rolls hit when total equals or exceeds Armor Class; natural 20 always hits and natural 1 always misses.
Attack rolls use the correct ability modifier and proficiency bonus for weapon attacks.
gaps: Weapon properties beyond ranged, finesse, thrown, and core SRD-backed profiles are not fully modeled.
rules.notes: Canonical weapon profiles and loadouts are preferred; missing weapon metadata is now enriched from SRD-backed fallback profiles.

rules.title: Poisoned creatures have Disadvantage on attack rolls.
Blinded attackers have Disadvantage and attacks against blinded targets have Advantage.
Prone creatures have Disadvantage on attacks; melee attacks against them have Advantage and ranged attacks against them have Disadvantage.
Incapacitated creatures can't take actions, Bonus Actions, or Reactions; the current backend blocks represented deterministic combat actions for incapacitated combatants.
gaps: The backend does not yet model the full action economy, so this rule currently gates the represented deterministic combat actions rather than every possible Action, Bonus Action, and Reaction.

rules.title: Ranged attacks beyond normal range and within long range have Disadvantage.
gaps: Range enforcement only applies when target distance is provided explicitly or can be derived from stored combatant positions.
Combatant positions can now be updated explicitly, but movement speed budgets, terrain, cover, and pathing are not yet modeled.
rules.notes: SRD-backed weapon fallback profiles now carry normal and long ranges for deterministic ranged attack checks.

rules.title: Reach weapons can make melee attacks out to 10 feet, and other melee attacks require the target to be within normal reach.
gaps: Reach enforcement only applies when target distance is provided explicitly or can be derived from stored combatant positions.
Combatant positions can now be updated explicitly, but opportunity attacks and other non-attack reach interactions are not yet modeled.

rules.title: Versatile weapons use their parenthetical damage value when used with two hands to make a melee attack.
gaps: Versatile damage currently depends on the caller explicitly setting use_two_hands or two_handed on the attack action.
Thrown versatile weapons like spears and tridents still share the repo's broader thrown-versus-ranged modeling gap.

rules.title: Light weapons can make one extra attack with a different Light weapon on the same turn, with the extra attack normally using a Bonus Action and not adding a positive ability modifier to damage.
gaps: The backend can now infer a Light extra attack from prior same-turn Light weapon attacks, infer Nick's Attack-action timing, and enforce a partial single-class Extra Attack slot model, but sequencing still relies on same-turn attack event history rather than a dedicated action resource.
The backend checks for a different Light weapon using prior event metadata and SRD-backed fallback profiles, but it does not yet model hand occupancy or item draw/stow interactions.
The broader Attack action and Extra Attack economy is still partial: multiclass non-stacking, warlock invocation variants, and non-attack-action extra attack sources are not yet modeled.

rules.title: Thrown melee weapons use melee attack ability logic in hand but become ranged attacks when thrown, using their thrown range.
gaps: Thrown attack mode is inferred from explicit action parameters or target distance; the backend does not yet model hand occupancy or explicit draw interactions.
Improvised throws of weapons without the Thrown property are not yet modeled.

rules.title: Loading weapons can fire only one piece of ammunition each time you use an action, Bonus Action, or Reaction to fire them.
gaps: Loading enforcement currently depends on the attack action carrying an explicit attack_timing value when the timing is not the default action.
The broader action-economy model does not yet distinguish Attack action multiattack flows from other sources of extra attacks.

rules.title: Ammunition weapons require one piece of the correct ammunition per attack, and after a fight half the expended ammunition can be recovered.
gaps: Ammunition enforcement currently applies only to canonical characters with tracked inventory, not free-standing NPC combatants.
The 1-minute post-fight recovery time is not modeled explicitly; recovery is exposed as a deterministic post-encounter action.
Recovery currently depends on encounter-linked attack events recorded by the backend action pipeline.

rules.title: Supported weapon mastery properties apply deterministic follow-up combat effects when the weapon profile explicitly enables mastery.
gaps: The backend currently supports cleave, graze, nick, push, sap, slow, topple, and vex mastery behaviors.
Mastery availability is explicit per weapon profile; class-level mastery selection and rest-based weapon choice are not yet modeled.
Cleave currently requires the caller to provide an explicit cleave_target_id and depends on stored combatant positions for reach and adjacency checks.
Nick now uses inferred Light-property follow-up attacks plus a partial Attack-action slot model, but it still depends on the repo's incomplete hand occupancy and broader action-economy modeling.

Push now enforces the Large-or-smaller target restriction when combatant size is present, but combatant size itself still defaults to explicit combatant input rather than broader character/species/transformation modeling.

Slow currently reduces the target's tracked combat speed and enforces the non-stacking 10-foot cap, but its timing expires on the slowed combatant's turn boundary rather than being modeled as a strict source-turn start effect.

Topple now uses explicit combatant saving throw bonuses when present, proficiency-aware default save bonuses for canonical character combatants, and a deterministic +0 save fallback for stat-light NPC combatants; monster stat blocks, monster save proficiencies, and size/immune edge cases are not yet modeled.

rules.title: Push mastery moves a Large or smaller target up to 10 feet away on a hit.
gaps: Push still depends on stored combatant positions being present before it can move the target; those positions can be initialized or updated explicitly, and missing-position blockers are reported in the attack outcome, but map occupancy, collision, and path blocking are not modeled.
Combatant size currently comes from explicit combatant input or a Medium default; it is not yet synchronized from broader character species, transformation, or monster data.

rules.title: Weapon damage dice and core longbow/longsword profiles align with the imported equipment rules.
gaps: Some mastery properties and class-level mastery selection rules are not yet enforced.
rules.notes: Core fallback damage dice, versatile damage values, ranged profiles, loading flags, ammunition item mappings, and SRD mastery-property metadata are aligned to the imported SRD weapons tables.

rules.title: Only the active combatant may take deterministic combat actions during an active encounter.
rules.notes: This is an app-level orchestration policy rather than a direct SRD chunk mapping.

rules.title: Combatant positions can be updated explicitly during an active encounter and are consumed by deterministic range and reach checks.
rules.notes: This is an app-level combat-state operation that makes existing range and reach enforcement usable after combat starts; it does not model movement action costs, terrain, cover, or pathing.

rules.title: The backend tracks turn-local attack timing state for action, Bonus Action, and Reaction attack sequencing, including Light extra attacks, Bonus Action attack caps, and loading timing buckets.
gaps: Turn-local attack timing and slot usage are derived from recorded same-turn attack events rather than from a fully modeled action-resource object.

The backend now exposes partial single-class Extra Attack slot state for action-timed primary attacks and enforces one Bonus Action attack per turn, but it still does not model hand occupancy, multiclass non-stacking, reaction refresh timing, or warlock invocation variants for attack sequencing.
rules.notes: Useful combat-state scaffolding for deterministic sequencing; Bonus Action attack limiting is an app-policy cap over represented attack_roll actions rather than a full action-resource engine.

rules.title: The Attack action grants a limited number of action-timed primary attacks, including single-class Extra Attack progression for supported martial classes.
gaps: The backend models covered single-class Fighter, Barbarian, Monk, Paladin, and Ranger Extra Attack progression only for action-timed primary attacks, with over-limit rejections reporting used and available Attack-action slots.
Nick extra attacks are excluded from the primary Attack-action slot count, but the broader action economy is still reconstructed from same-turn attack events rather than a dedicated turn resource.
Multiclass non-stacking, warlock invocation variants like Thirsting Blade and Devouring Blade, and non-class sources of additional Attack-action attacks are not yet modeled.

rules.title: Temporary AC, attack, damage, and speed modifiers can be applied as timed combat effects.
gaps: Effects are generic app abstractions; Shield of Faith and Barkskin now have named SRD-backed actions, but most spells, class features, and items still use the generic substrate or are not modeled.
Concentration damage saves and save-to-end behavior are not yet modeled.
Concentration cleanup currently applies only to effects explicitly marked as requiring concentration.
Combat speed is tracked only in active encounters; the backend now supports explicit position updates but still lacks a broader movement action-cost system.

rules.title: Shield of Faith grants a +2 AC bonus to a target within 60 feet while the caster maintains concentration.
gaps: The named cast action applies the SRD +2 AC bonus, marks the effect as concentration, and rejects known targets beyond 60 feet.
Spell slot spending, casting components, spell preparation, Bonus Action spell timing, willing-target checks, and exact 10-minute duration tracking are not yet modeled.
Concentration damage saves and broader concentration ending triggers are not yet modeled.

rules.title: Barkskin gives a touched willing creature Armor Class 17 if its AC is lower.
gaps: The named cast action applies an AC floor of 17, preserves already-higher AC values, and rejects known targets beyond touch range.
The backend represents touch range as 5 feet when distance is known.
Spell slot spending, casting components, spell preparation, Bonus Action spell timing, willing-target checks, and exact 1-hour duration tracking are not yet modeled.

rules.title: Combat ends automatically when only one side has conscious combatants remaining.
rules.notes: Useful app automation; not currently mapped to a specific SRD chunk.
